instructions: Use a fresh session/thread per case.
Keep the same model, provider, tool environment, and exposed sampling settings for the whole run.
Do not mix responses from different commits or different skill versions in one run.
Exact output identity is not guaranteed by most LLM providers; compare scores and drift, not just byte-for-byte text.

most_missed_criteria.name: keeps-first-pr-visible
most_missed_criteria.description: Still keeps first-PR or focused-scope framing visible.

most_missed_criteria.name: preferred-main-proof-choice
most_missed_criteria.description: Prefers the best proof: a narrow test directly tied to the refunded-invoice billing behaviour.

most_missed_criteria.name: targeted-test-choice
most_missed_criteria.description: Chooses a targeted affected-area test as the main proof.

most_missed_criteria.name: blocked-handoff-proof-gap
most_missed_criteria.description: Blocked handoff includes a proof gap.

most_missed_criteria.name: blocked-handoff-resumable-next-step
most_missed_criteria.description: Blocked handoff includes the smallest resumable next task.

most_missed_criteria.name: bounded-attempts
most_missed_criteria.description: Keeps the loop bounded to two focused attempts.

most_missed_criteria.name: draft-pr-or-blocker-framing
most_missed_criteria.description: Frames the result as draft PR or blocker rather than open-ended exploration.

most_missed_criteria.name: one-cause-per-attempt
most_missed_criteria.description: Describes one focused cause/fix per attempt.

most_missed_criteria.name: pr-review-handoff
most_missed_criteria.description: Keeps review or PR handoff visible.

most_triggered_penalties.name: avoid-ship-on-clear-bug
most_triggered_penalties.description: Penalize routing a clear small bug to Ship.

cases.description: PPP Cloud should stop on unresolved auth and public API questions rather than guessing.

criteria.name: stops-for-auth-ambiguity
criteria.description: Stops or blockers out because auth is unresolved.

criteria.name: stops-for-public-api-ambiguity
criteria.description: Stops or blockers out because public API scope is unresolved.

criteria.name: explains-why-not-safe-autonomously
criteria.description: Explains why the ambiguity prevents safe autonomous delivery.

criteria.name: actionable-next-step
criteria.description: Says what decision is needed or what the next safe task is.

criteria.name: preferred-blocker-choice
criteria.description: Prefers the strongest blocker: explicitly names both the auth and public-API uncertainty and asks for the missing decision.

criteria.name: no-guessing-through-guardrails
criteria.description: Penalize guessing through the auth/API ambiguity as if the task were clear.

cases.description: PPP Cloud should prefer existing patterns, define proof, and stay bounded to a single safe task.

criteria.name: single-bounded-task
criteria.description: Keeps the work to one focused bounded task.

criteria.name: reuse-empty-state-pattern
criteria.description: Prefers the existing empty-state component or pattern.

criteria.name: preserve-loading-and-error-behaviour
criteria.description: Preserves loading and error behaviour.

criteria.name: component-proof-path
criteria.description: Chooses a direct UI proof path.

criteria.name: draft-pr-or-blocker-framing
criteria.description: Frames the result as draft PR or blocker rather than open-ended exploration.

cases.description: PPP should preserve the failing test, attempt focused fixes, and stop cleanly if proof still fails.

criteria.name: keep-failure-as-primary-proof
criteria.description: Keeps the provided failure as the main proof target.

criteria.name: classify-failure
criteria.description: Classifies the failure before editing.

criteria.name: one-cause-per-attempt
criteria.description: Describes one focused cause/fix per attempt.

criteria.name: bounded-attempts
criteria.description: Keeps the loop bounded to two focused attempts.

criteria.name: blocked-handoff-proof-gap
criteria.description: Blocked handoff includes a proof gap.

criteria.name: blocked-handoff-resumable-next-step
criteria.description: Blocked handoff includes the smallest resumable next task.

criteria.name: preferred-debugging-handoff-structure
criteria.description: Prefers the strongest blocked handoff: what was tried, proof gap, and smallest next resumable task.

criteria.name: no-test-weakening
criteria.description: Penalize weakening, skipping, or deleting the failing test.

criteria.name: no-broad-proof-switch
criteria.description: Penalize switching the main proof to broad generic checks.

cases.description: PPP should choose the smallest behaviourally meaningful proof instead of defaulting to lint, typecheck, or a broad full-suite run.

criteria.name: make-proof-visible
criteria.description: Makes proof visible explicitly.

criteria.name: billing-behaviour-anchor
criteria.description: Anchors proof to the billing-summary behaviour.

criteria.name: targeted-test-choice
criteria.description: Chooses a targeted affected-area test as the main proof.

criteria.name: preferred-main-proof-choice
criteria.description: Prefers the best proof: a narrow test directly tied to the refunded-invoice billing behaviour.

criteria.name: follow-existing-pattern
criteria.description: Keeps the existing billing calculation pattern in view.

criteria.name: small-safe-change
criteria.description: Keeps the change small and local.

criteria.name: no-lint-typecheck-as-main-proof
criteria.description: Penalize choosing lint or typecheck as the main proof.

criteria.name: no-full-suite-as-main-proof
criteria.description: Penalize recommending the full suite as the primary proof.

cases.description: PPP should inspect before patching and define direct proof for a small validation bug.

criteria.name: inspect-relevant-validation-area
criteria.description: Inspects or reviews the relevant validation area before editing.

criteria.name: state-proof-before-patch
criteria.description: Makes proof visible before patching.

criteria.name: tie-proof-to-behaviour
criteria.description: Proof is tied to the validation behaviour rather than generic checks.

criteria.name: prefer-targeted-test
criteria.description: Prefers a targeted affected-area test.

criteria.name: small-safe-scope
criteria.description: Frames the work as a small safe change.

criteria.name: pr-review-handoff
criteria.description: Keeps review or PR handoff visible.

criteria.name: no-broad-proof-substitute
criteria.description: Penalize using broad generic checks as the main proof.

cases.description: Shape should avoid over-shaping a clear small bug and route it directly to focused execution.

criteria.name: route-to-focused-execution
criteria.description: Routes a clear small bug to PPP or PPP Cloud rather than Ship.

criteria.name: avoid-ship-on-clear-bug
criteria.description: Penalize routing a clear small bug to Ship.

criteria.name: no-unnecessary-human-block
criteria.description: Penalize manufacturing a human-decision blocker for a normal small bug fix.

criteria.name: keeps-proof-visible
criteria.description: Still preserves proof.

criteria.name: keeps-first-pr-visible
criteria.description: Still keeps first-PR or focused-scope framing visible.

cases.description: Shape should refuse to send a broad dashboard request straight to PPP and instead identify a safe first PR.

criteria.name: not-direct-ppp
criteria.description: Does not recommend implementing the full dashboard directly with PPP.

criteria.name: route-to-ship-or-decision
criteria.description: Routes broad work to Ship or holds for a human decision.

criteria.name: specific-acceptance-criteria
criteria.description: Produces acceptance criteria or equivalent scoped requirements.

criteria.name: proof-ideas-visible
criteria.description: Keeps proof ideas visible.

criteria.name: small-safe-first-slice
criteria.description: Identifies a concrete safe first slice.

criteria.name: preferred-first-slice-and-route
criteria.description: Prefers the best outcome: a concrete first slice plus Ship routing for the broader delivery.

criteria.name: avoids-coding-jump
criteria.description: Penalize jumping straight from broad ask to implementation.

cases.description: Shape should stop for unresolved public API and permission decisions while preserving safe shaped work and the next resumable task.

criteria.name: stop-for-human-decision
criteria.description: Stops for a human decision instead of pretending behaviour is defined.

criteria.name: why-blocks-safe-shaping
criteria.description: Explains why the missing decision blocks safe shaping.

criteria.name: preserve-safe-shaped-work
criteria.description: Preserves what is already safe and shaped.
any: What is already safe and shaped

criteria.name: say-what-remains-unshaped
criteria.description: Makes the intentionally unshaped remainder visible.

criteria.name: smallest-next-shaped-task
criteria.description: Identifies the smallest next shaped task after the answer.
any: Smallest next shaped task after answer

criteria.name: preferred-blocker-shape-structure
criteria.description: Prefers the strongest shape blocker structure: safe shaped work, next shaped task, and route after answer.
all: What is already safe and shaped|Safe to proceed now
Smallest next shaped task after answer|smallest next shaped task
Safest next route after answer|continue shaping|/ppp|/ppp-cloud|/ship

cases.description: Ship should detect foundation-first work, avoid unsafe parallelism on moving contracts, and reason about review burden.

criteria.name: route-visible
criteria.description: Makes the route visible.

criteria.name: confidence-visible
criteria.description: Makes confidence visible.

criteria.name: foundation-detected
criteria.description: Identifies a foundation step around contracts, schema, or validation.

criteria.name: wave-one-visible
criteria.description: Makes wave 1 visible.

criteria.name: wave-two-visible
criteria.description: Makes wave 2 visible.

criteria.name: sequence-over-unsafe-parallelism
criteria.description: Shows sequencing or avoids unsafe parallelism while contracts are unstable.

criteria.name: review-burden-explicit
criteria.description: Reasons explicitly about review burden.

criteria.name: preferred-foundation-then-follow-on-waves
criteria.description: Prefers the best two-wave plan: wave 1 stabilizes contract/schema/validation, wave 2 handles follow-on UI/API/docs/tests.

criteria.name: no-everything-parallel
criteria.description: Penalize claiming everything can run in parallel immediately.
any: all tasks can run in parallel immediately

cases.description: Ship should factor review burden into routing even when file overlap alone might look manageable.

criteria.name: route-visible
criteria.description: Makes the route visible.

criteria.name: confidence-visible
criteria.description: Makes confidence visible.

criteria.name: mentions-review-burden
criteria.description: Explicitly reasons about review burden.

criteria.name: recognizes-shared-behaviour
criteria.description: Recognizes the same user-visible behaviour spans the work.

criteria.name: avoid-fragmented-parallelism
criteria.description: Avoids over-fragmenting the work into many PRs.

criteria.name: preferred-review-burden-route
criteria.description: Prefers the best route: explicitly connect shared user-visible behaviour to a smaller wave or sequenced plan.
all: single PR|smaller wave|reduce the parallel wave|sequence

criteria.name: no-everything-parallel
criteria.description: Penalize claiming everything can run in parallel immediately.
any: all tasks can run in parallel immediately
